column1: # Publishes OCL results keyed on functional annotation groups (annogroups)
# rather than orthogroups. Mirrors the orthogroups_X_ocl manifest structure.
# When workflow-COPYME-ocl_analysis is copied to workflow-RUN_N-ocl_analysis,
# this manifest travels with it. Flip lines to `yes` to publish.
# Destination (auto-derived by shared helper; the run fans out PER SOURCE, so the
# source is preserved in the dest path):
# The leading OUTPUT_pipeline/*/ glob matches every source (pfam, go, panther, ...).
# Annogroups are IMPORTED from the annogroups subproject (four-type framework:
# feature, combination, architecture, absent). OCL maps the origin-bearing types
# (absent excluded) onto each structure, so the per-type OCL summaries are
# feature / combination / architecture. Script 001 emits annogroup_map +
# annogroups-species_identifiers only (no per-type 1-output tables, no subtypes
# manifest -- those were the old single/combo/zero scheme).
# Policy: each subproject hosts its OWN outputs only. The phylogenetic
# clade_mappings) are INPUTS copied from trees_species; they are hosted under

column6: description

column4: Annogroup tree counts — species-tree deconvolution (member species)
column6: Species-tree deconvolution for this structure: per annogroup (feature, combination, architecture; absent excluded), the member-SPECIES count within every clade of the structure, one column per clade ordered root to tips. A full-coverage (root) clade equals the annogroup's species count.

column4: Annogroup tree counts — species-tree deconvolution (member proteins)
column6: The companion member-PROTEIN counts per clade for this structure, imported from the annogroups subproject's per-structure tree-counts (annogroups owns sequence membership). Same clade columns as the species table.

One row per CURATED composite clade (from INPUT_user/composite_clades_manifest.tsv): its identifier, the algorithm, its definition, and the count of annogroups that match it. Computed ONCE for the whole run (annogroup member species are stable across structures, Rule 6). A composite clade is a user-defined question about where an annogroup's member species fall on the species tree, evaluated by one of four algorithms: exact (members from EXACTLY the listed components), absent (members absent from ALL listed clades), core_urclade (members in an outgroup of the target AND an ingroup; the target's Ur=last-common-ancestor core), core_early_clade (members in two or more ingroups; the target's Early=early descendant branches). Default building blocks: the five metazoan clades Ctenophora Porifera Placozoa Cnidaria Bilateria within Metazoa, plus NonMetazoa for members outside Metazoa.

Every annogroup with one column per algorithm: its exact composite clade (one-per-annogroup, when curated, else None), and the comma lists of absent / core_urclade / core_early_clade composite clades it matches (independent tests, so an annogroup can match several), else None.

column4: Composite clade detail tables — annogroup identifiers, definitions, member species
column6: One table per curated composite clade: each row an annogroup that matches it, with its annotation definitions and, in one column per relevant clade, the member SPECIES there (OCL works at the species level, so detail columns list species rather than sequences).
